steps.title: Substitute the input
steps.content:
Substitute $x=\frac{1}{3}$ into the function. This input is useful because it makes the quantity inside the squared part equal to zero.
$$f(\frac{1}{3})=6(\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{3})^2+\frac{7}{3}$$

steps.title: Use the zero square
steps.content:
The squared part becomes $0$, so the first term contributes nothing to the value of the function.
$$f(\frac{1}{3})=6(0)^2+\frac{7}{3}=\frac{7}{3}$$
The constant term is all that remains. The answer is 7/3.
